name: Homes On The Hill
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counseling agency in southwestern Franklin County, Ohio, offering free financial and housing education to homebuyers, homeowners, and renters.
description_long:
Homes on the Hill Community Development Corporation (HOTH-CDC) is a Columbus-area nonprofit established in 1993 with nearly 30 years of experience in affordable housing development and counseling. Incorporated as a 501(c)(3) organization, the agency has developed over 100 owner-occupied and rental housing units and partnered with Habitat for Humanity to build more than 30 additional homes in the Hilltop neighborhood and southwestern Franklin County.

The organization provides comprehensive free services across multiple consumer segments: HUD-approved homeownership education and individual counseling for homebuyers, foreclosure prevention orientations, homeowner counseling, and renter support programs including occasional rental assistance through their Landlord Engagement Action Network (LEAN). All services are delivered by HUD-certified counselors in both English and Spanish, with no fees charged to ensure accessibility for those most in need.

HOTH-CDC distinguishes itself through its dual focus on education and direct housing development—the organization doesn't simply counsel families but actively builds and rehabilitates affordable properties in the community. Their 30-year track record in southwestern Ohio, combined with HUD approval status and multilingual counselor availability, positions them as a deeply embedded local resource. They also serve as a referral partner for lenders and realtors seeking professional counseling services for clients.

As a nonprofit dependent on grants and donations, HOTH-CDC's capacity may vary by program availability, particularly for rental assistance. Their geographic focus is limited to southwestern Franklin County rather than statewide, though they are HUD-approved to serve all Ohio residents. The organization's service quality is inherently tied to their nonprofit funding model, which creates sustainability but not growth guarantees.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency with certified counselors
All services provided completely free of charge
Nearly 30 years of direct experience in affordable housing development and community work
Services available in English and Spanish
Comprehensive service spectrum covering homebuyers, homeowners, and renters
Offers foreclosure prevention-specific orientations in addition to standard counseling
Active property development provides real community housing solutions beyond education
cons: Geographic service area limited to southwestern Franklin County, though HUD-approved for all Ohio residents
Rental assistance availability is described as occasional, suggesting inconsistent availability
As nonprofit dependent on grants and donations, service capacity and program availability may fluctuate
Website provides limited detail on wait times, counselor accessibility, or specific program outcomes
No indication of virtual counseling availability or service delivery flexibility
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking free HUD-approved education in Franklin County
Homeowners facing foreclosure or seeking financial counseling
Spanish-speaking residents needing financial or housing education
Renters seeking landlord dispute resolution and rental assistance resources
diagnosis: Homes on the Hill is best for southwestern Ohio residents—particularly first-time homebuyers, homeowners in financial distress, and renters—seeking free, trusted, HUD-approved housing counseling backed by 30 years of local community work. The main caveat is geographic limitation to southwestern Franklin County and reliance on grant funding, which may affect program availability and wait times.
services: Occasional rental assistance through LEAN (Landlord Engagement Action Network)
Affordable housing property development and rehabilitation
Bilingual services in English and Spanish
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
